text: Attention is all you need. Transformer architecture uses self-attention to achieve state-of-the-art in machine translation.
We present a neural network architecture that learns to deduce lower-level representations from high-level abstract proofs.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing in human embryos shows high off-target rates. We sequenced 10 embryos and found 15% unintended mutations.
Quantum entanglement experiments demonstrate Bell inequality violations in photons at 500m separation.
High-temperature superconductivity observed in hydrogen sulfide under 200 GPa pressure at 203 K.
The Large Hadron Collider confirms the existence of the Higgs boson with mass ~125 GeV/c^2.
AlphaFold predicts protein structures at atomic accuracy, revolutionizing computational biology.
We propose a method for deep reinforcement learning to achieve superhuman performance in Go and chess.
Observation of gravitational waves from binary black hole merger GW150914.
A method for RNA sequencing enables single-cell resolution transcriptomics across tissues.
The Standard Model predicts gauge bosons W+, W-, and Z0 with high accuracy, confirmed experimentally.
Neural networks can approximate any continuous function according to the universal approximation theorem.
Metamaterials enable negative refractive index, allowing superlens imaging below diffraction limit.
A method to train LLMs with self-supervised learning on text-only datasets to achieve general language understanding.
Observation of neutrino oscillations confirms that neutrinos have mass.
Bayesian inference methods applied to cosmological parameters yield precise estimates of dark matter density.
Deep generative models like VAEs and GANs can synthesize realistic images and audio.
Single-molecule CRISPR imaging allows live-cell tracking of DNA editing in real time.
Observational cosmology shows accelerated expansion of the universe, consistent with dark energy models.
Self-supervised contrastive learning improves image representation quality on multiple computer vision benchmarks.
The Drake equation estimates the number of technologically advanced civilizations in the galaxy.
Topological insulators exhibit conducting edge states protected by time-reversal symmetry.
Reinforcement learning agents can learn to play Atari games from raw pixel input without supervision.
RNA-seq analysis shows differential expression patterns between healthy and diseased tissues.
The LIGO interferometer uses laser interferometry to detect spacetime distortions from gravitational waves.
Variational quantum algorithms are applied to simulate molecular energies on near-term quantum computers.
Graph neural networks model relational data in chemistry, physics, and social networks effectively.
Single-cell transcriptomics allows mapping of cell types in the developing human brain.
Transformers achieve state-of-the-art results in protein folding prediction tasks.
Topological quantum computing uses non-Abelian anyons for fault-tolerant qubits.
Diffusion models enable high-quality generative image synthesis surpassing GANs in diversity and realism.
CRISPR base editors allow precise single-nucleotide editing without double-strand breaks.
theorem sqrt2_irrational: "sqrt 2 ∉ ℚ" proof assume "sqrt 2 ∈ ℚ" ... qed
∀ ε > 0, ∃ N ∈ ℕ such that ∀ n ≥ N, |a_n - L| < ε. This is the definition of a convergent sequence.
Euler's formula: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
The Riemann Hypothesis states that all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function have real part 1/2.
Prove by induction: sum_{i=1}^n i = n(n+1)/2.
Definition of a group: (G, *) is a group if closure, associativity, identity, and inverse properties hold.
Proof that there are infinitely many primes: assume finite, construct product +1, leads to contradiction.
Definition: A metric space (X, d) satisfies positivity, symmetry, triangle inequality, and d(x, y) = 0 iff x = y.
Cantor's diagonalization proof shows that the real numbers are uncountable.
Fundamental theorem of calculus: If f is continuous on [a, b], then ∫_a^b f'(x) dx = f(b) - f(a).
The Pythagorean theorem: In a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2.
Definition: A topology on a set X is a collection of subsets T ⊆ P(X) closed under union and finite intersection.
Proof that e is irrational: assume e = p/q, expand as series, leads to contradiction.
Lagrange multipliers provide necessary conditions for constrained local extrema of functions.
Definition: A field is a set F with two operations (+, *) satisfying closure, associativity, distributivity, identity, and inverses.
Taylor series: f(x) = Σ (f^n(a)/n!)*(x-a)^n converges to f(x) under certain conditions.
The axiom of choice: For any set of non-empty sets, there exists a choice function selecting one element from each set.
Fermat's Little Theorem: If p is prime and a ∈ ℤ, then a^p ≡ a (mod p).
Definition: A vector space V over a field F has vector addition and scalar multiplication satisfying 8 axioms.
Proof: The sum of the angles of a triangle in Euclidean space equals 180°.
Definition: An inner product space is a vector space with a positive-definite bilinear form ⟨·,·⟩.
Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟨x,y⟩| ≤ ||x||·||y|| for all vectors x, y in inner product space.
Definition: A normed space is a vector space with a function ||·|| satisfying positivity, scalar multiplication, and triangle inequality.
Proof: The derivative of sin(x) is cos(x) using limit definition of derivative.
Definition: A sequence {a_n} is Cauchy if ∀ ε>0 ∃ N s.t. m,n ≥ N ⇒ |a_m - a_n|<ε.
Fundamental theorem of algebra: Every non-constant polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root.
Definition: A linear transformation T: V→W satisfies T(v + u) = T(v) + T(u) and T(c·v) = c·T(v).
Binomial theorem: (x + y)^n = Σ_{k=0}^n (n choose k) x^{n-k} y^k.
Definition: A topology is Hausdorff if any two distinct points have disjoint neighborhoods.
Definition: A differentiable manifold is a topological manifold with a smooth structure.
Green's theorem: ∮_C (L dx + M dy) = ∬_D (∂M/∂x - ∂L/∂y) dA.
Stokes' theorem: ∮_∂S F·dr = ∬_S (∇×F)·dS.
Euler's formula: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
The Riemann Hypothesis states that all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function have real part 1/2.
Prove by induction: sum_{i=1}^n i = n(n+1)/2.
∀ ε > 0, ∃ N ∈ ℕ such that ∀ n ≥ N, |a_n - L| < ε. This is the definition of a convergent sequence.
Cantor’s diagonal argument proves that the set of real numbers is uncountable.
The Law of Quadratic Reciprocity describes the solvability of quadratic equations modulo prime numbers.
Fermat’s Last Theorem: No three positive integers a, b, c satisfy a^n + b^n = c^n for n > 2.
The Banach–Tarski paradox shows a solid sphere can be decomposed and reassembled into two identical spheres.
The Fourier Transform converts a function from its time domain to frequency domain: F(ω) = ∫ f(t)e^(-iωt) dt.
The Poincaré Conjecture: Every simply connected, closed 3-manifold is homeomorphic to the 3-sphere.
The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟨u, v⟩| ≤ ||u||·||v|| for vectors in an inner product space.
Lagrange’s four-square theorem: every natural number is the sum of four integer squares.
The Fundamental Theorem of Algebra states every non-zero single-variable polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root.
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus links differentiation and integration: ∫_a^b f'(x) dx = f(b) - f(a).
Gödel’s incompleteness theorem: any consistent system capable of arithmetic has true statements that are unprovable.
A proof by induction example: sum_{i=1}^n i^2 = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6.
Taylor series expansion of e^x: e^x = Σ (x^n)/n! for n=0 to ∞.
Maclaurin series for sin(x): sin(x) = Σ (-1)^n x^(2n+1)/(2n+1)! for n=0 to ∞.
The Gamma function generalizes the factorial: Γ(n) = ∫_0^∞ x^(n-1)e^(-x) dx.
The zeta function ζ(s) = Σ n^-s encodes the distribution of prime numbers in its zeros.
The Prime Number Theorem: π(n) ~ n / ln(n), describing the asymptotic distribution of primes.
The Lagrange interpolation formula reconstructs a polynomial through n+1 points.
The Legendre polynomials P_n(x) solve Legendre’s differential equation.
The Hermite polynomials H_n(x) appear in solutions to the quantum harmonic oscillator.
The Chebyshev polynomials T_n(x) minimize the maximum error in polynomial approximation.
The Bessel function J_n(x) solves Bessel’s differential equation: x^2 y'' + x y' + (x^2-n^2)y=0.
The Laplace transform: F(s) = ∫_0^∞ e^(-st) f(t) dt, converting functions from time to complex frequency domain.
The Möbius function μ(n) is defined as 0 if n has squared prime factor, (-1)^k if n is product of k distinct primes.
Euler’s totient function φ(n) counts integers up to n that are coprime to n.
The Dirichlet theorem on arithmetic progressions: every arithmetic progression a, a+d, a+2d,... with gcd(a,d)=1 contains infinitely many primes.
Stirling’s approximation: n! ≈ sqrt(2πn) (n/e)^n, useful for asymptotics.
The Gamma function generalizes factorials: Γ(n) = ∫_0^∞ x^(n-1) e^(-x) dx.
The Weierstrass function is continuous everywhere but differentiable nowhere.
The Cantor set is uncountable, totally disconnected, and has measure zero.
The Heine-Borel theorem: a subset of ℝ^n is compact iff it is closed and bounded.
The Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem: every bounded sequence in ℝ^n has a convergent subsequence.
The Rouché’s theorem gives conditions for the number of zeros of complex functions within a contour.
Liouville’s theorem: every bounded entire function must be constant.
The Cauchy integral formula provides the value of a holomorphic function inside a contour in terms of its values on the contour.
The residue theorem evaluates complex line integrals using residues of poles inside the contour.
Schur’s lemma: in representation theory, a linear map commuting with irreducible representations is scalar.
The Jordan canonical form represents a linear operator by a block-diagonal matrix of Jordan blocks.
The spectral theorem: every normal operator on a finite-dimensional complex vector space is unitarily diagonalizable.
The Gram-Schmidt process orthonormalizes a set of vectors in an inner product space.
The Frobenius norm of a matrix is the square root of the sum of the absolute squares of its elements.
The Cayley-Hamilton theorem: every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation.
The singular value decomposition expresses any matrix as UΣV^T with orthogonal U, V and diagonal Σ.
The Hadamard inequality bounds the determinant of a matrix in terms of the norms of its rows.
The Gershgorin circle theorem gives bounds on eigenvalues of a square matrix.
The Riemann mapping theorem: any non-empty simply connected open subset of ℂ (not all ℂ) is conformally equivalent to the unit disk.
Montel’s theorem: a family of holomorphic functions that is uniformly bounded on every compact subset is normal.
The Schwarz lemma: a holomorphic map from the unit disk to itself fixing 0 is a contraction.
The Liouville-Rudin theorem: bounded entire functions are constant (generalization of Liouville).
The Mittag-Leffler theorem constructs meromorphic functions with prescribed poles.
Runge’s theorem: any holomorphic function on a compact set with connected complement can be approximated uniformly by rational functions.
The Baire category theorem: in a complete metric space, countable intersections of dense open sets are dense.
The Banach fixed-point theorem: any contraction mapping on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point.
Hahn-Banach theorem: every bounded linear functional defined on a subspace extends to the whole space without increasing its norm.
Open mapping theorem: a surjective continuous linear map between Banach spaces is open.
Uniform boundedness principle: pointwise bounded family of continuous linear operators is uniformly bounded.
Closed graph theorem: a linear operator between Banach spaces is bounded iff its graph is closed.
The Riesz representation theorem: every continuous linear functional on a Hilbert space can be represented as an inner product with a fixed vector.
Stone-Weierstrass theorem: any continuous function on a closed interval can be uniformly approximated by polynomials.
The Sobolev embedding theorem provides conditions under which Sobolev spaces embed continuously into spaces of continuous functions.
Attention is all you need. Transformer architecture uses self-attention to achieve state-of-the-art in machine translation.
We present a neural network architecture that learns to deduce lower-level representations from high-level abstract proofs.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing in human embryos shows high off-target rates. We sequenced 10 embryos and found 15% unintended mutations.
Deep reinforcement learning achieves superhuman performance in complex games using reward maximization.
AlphaFold predicts 3D protein structures from amino acid sequences with high accuracy.
Quantum teleportation transfers quantum states between distant particles using entanglement.
Lattice QCD calculations provide non-perturbative insights into strong nuclear interactions.
Graph neural networks extend deep learning to graph-structured data for node and edge predictions.
The Higgs boson discovery confirms the Standard Model's mechanism for particle mass generation.
Topological insulators exhibit surface conductivity while remaining insulating in the bulk.
Black hole information paradox challenges the reconciliation of quantum mechanics and general relativity.
Neural ordinary differential equations generalize residual networks to continuous-depth models.
Generative adversarial networks (GANs) train two networks in a minimax game to generate realistic data.
Transformer-XL extends Transformers with recurrence to capture long-term dependencies.
BERT: bidirectional encoder representations from Transformers pre-train deep bidirectional models for NLP.
The Standard Model describes electromagnetic, weak, and strong interactions in particle physics.
Photonic crystals control the propagation of light using periodic dielectric structures.
Quantum error correction protects quantum information from decoherence and operational errors.
Neural Turing Machines combine neural networks with external memory for algorithmic tasks.
Wave-particle duality: quantum entities exhibit both wave-like and particle-like behavior.
Quantum key distribution enables secure communication based on quantum mechanics principles.
Spiking neural networks model neuron firing times and capture temporal information.
The AdS/CFT correspondence relates gravity in anti-de Sitter space to a conformal field theory on the boundary.
AlphaZero learns board games from self-play without human data, achieving superhuman performance.
Topological quantum computing uses anyons and braiding to perform fault-tolerant quantum operations.
Differentiable programming generalizes neural networks by allowing gradients through arbitrary code.
Quantum supremacy demonstrates computational tasks infeasible for classical computers.
Self-attention in Transformers allows the model to weigh all input tokens globally at each layer.
Neural architecture search automates finding optimal neural network topologies.
Few-shot learning enables models to generalize from a small number of labeled examples.
Graph attention networks leverage attention mechanisms on nodes for improved graph representation learning.
Energy-based models define a scalar energy for each configuration and learn to assign low energy to observed data.
Self-supervised learning pretrains models using unlabeled data and downstream fine-tuning.
Contrastive learning trains representations by distinguishing similar and dissimilar pairs of data.
Attention mechanisms can be applied to images to improve visual recognition tasks.
Variational autoencoders model latent variables with a probabilistic encoder-decoder architecture.
The Navier-Stokes equations describe the motion of viscous fluid substances.
Phase transitions occur when a system changes from one state of matter to another, e.g., solid to liquid.
Reinforcement learning with policy gradients optimizes stochastic policies directly.
Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks solve vanishing gradient problems in RNNs.
The Fourier series represents periodic functions as sums of sines and cosines.
Schrödinger equation predicts how the quantum state of a physical system evolves over time.
The Monte Carlo method uses random sampling to estimate numerical solutions to problems.
Boltzmann machines learn probability distributions over binary-valued data using energy-based modeling.
The PageRank algorithm ranks web pages based on link structure and eigenvector centrality.
The backpropagation algorithm efficiently computes gradients for multilayer neural networks.
Stochastic gradient descent optimizes model parameters by computing gradients on mini-batches of data.
Capsule networks aim to preserve hierarchical pose relationships between features.
Elastic weight consolidation reduces catastrophic forgetting in continual learning.
Deep Q-Networks combine deep learning with Q-learning for end-to-end reinforcement learning.
Hopfield networks store associative memories and retrieve them via energy minimization.
Bayesian neural networks represent uncertainty by placing distributions over weights.
The Ising model explains phase transitions in ferromagnetic materials using spin interactions.
Diffusion models generate data by reversing a gradual noising process to produce realistic samples.
Neural style transfer modifies images by recombining content and style representations in deep networks.
The Hopf fibration maps S^3 to S^2, providing a topological structure with fibered circles.
Convolutional neural networks apply spatial filters to exploit local correlations in data.
Attention is used in vision transformers to model relationships between image patches.
Reversible neural networks allow exact reconstruction of inputs from outputs, saving memory in training.
Attention biasing can improve performance in multi-modal transformer architectures.
Neural ODEs unify residual networks and continuous-time dynamical systems for modeling trajectories.
Physics-informed neural networks encode differential equation constraints into deep learning models.
Hyperbolic neural networks use hyperbolic geometry to embed hierarchical data efficiently.
Quantum annealing leverages quantum tunneling to find low-energy states of combinatorial optimization problems.

theorem sqrt2_irrational: "sqrt 2 ∉ ℚ"
proof
 assume "sqrt 2 ∈ ℚ" ... qed
∀ ε > 0, ∃ N ∈ ℕ such that ∀ n ≥ N, |a_n - L| < ε. This is the definition of a convergent sequence.
Euler's formula: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
The Riemann Hypothesis states that all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function have real part 1/2.
The Fourier Transform converts a time-domain signal into its frequency components.
Prove by induction: sum_{i=1}^n i = n(n+1)/2.
The Fibonacci sequence is defined recursively as F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).
Linear algebra: a matrix A has an inverse A^-1 if and only if det(A) ≠ 0.
Taylor series: f(x) = Σ (f^n(a)/n!) * (x-a)^n around point a.
Complex numbers: z = a + bi, where i^2 = -1.
Group theory: a set G with operation • is a group if it satisfies closure, associativity, identity, and inverses.
Ring theory: a set R with two operations + and * is a ring if (R,+) is an abelian group and * is associative.
Vector calculus: the gradient of a scalar field f is ∇f = (∂f/∂x, ∂f/∂y, ∂f/∂z).
Differential equation example: dy/dx = ky ⇒ y = Ce^(kx).
Eigenvalues and eigenvectors: Av = λv.
Probability theory: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B).
Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).
Combinatorics: C(n,k) = n! / (k!(n-k)!).
Set theory: A ∩ B = {x | x ∈ A and x ∈ B}.
Logic: (P ∧ Q) ⇒ R.
Mathematical induction: if true for n=0 and n⇒n+1, then true for all n∈ℕ.
Topology: a set X with open sets τ such that ∅,X ∈ τ and arbitrary unions and finite intersections are in τ.
Metric space: d(x,y) satisfies positivity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
Linear programming: maximize c^T x subject to Ax ≤ b.
Graph theory: a graph G = (V,E) consists of vertices V and edges E.
Differential geometry: study of curves and surfaces using calculus.
Four color theorem: any planar map can be colored with ≤4 colors such that adjacent regions differ.
Pythagorean theorem: in a right triangle, a^2 + b^2 = c^2.
Complex analysis: f(z) analytic ⇒ Cauchy-Riemann equations hold.
Fourier series convergence: if f is piecewise smooth, its Fourier series converges to f(x).
Rolle's theorem: if f(a)=f(b) and f differentiable, ∃ c∈(a,b) such that f'(c)=0.
Mean Value Theorem: if f continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), ∃ c∈(a,b) s.t. f'(c) = (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a).
Cauchy's integral theorem: ∮_C f(z) dz = 0 if f analytic in simply connected domain containing C.
Stokes' theorem: ∫_S curl F · dS = ∮_∂S F · dr.
Green's theorem: ∮_C (P dx + Q dy) = ∬_R (∂Q/∂x - ∂P/∂y) dA.
Divergence theorem: ∭_V div F dV = ∬_∂V F · n dS.
Lebesgue integral generalizes the Riemann integral by measuring the size of sets where function values occur.
Fourier transform properties: linearity, shift, scaling, convolution.
Laplace transform: L{f(t)} = ∫_0^∞ e^(-st) f(t) dt.
Z-transform: Z{a_n} = Σ a_n z^(-n) for discrete-time signals.
Spectral theorem: every normal operator on finite-dimensional complex vector space is diagonalizable.
Jordan canonical form: every square matrix is similar to a block diagonal matrix with Jordan blocks.
Singular value decomposition: A = U Σ V^T, where U,V orthogonal and Σ diagonal with non-negative entries.
Cayley-Hamilton theorem: every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation.
Euler characteristic χ = V - E + F for polyhedra.
Binomial theorem: (x+y)^n = Σ (n choose k) x^(n-k) y^k.
Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟨u,v⟩| ≤ ||u|| ||v||.
Triangle inequality: ||x+y|| ≤ ||x|| + ||y||.
Contraction mapping theorem: a contraction on complete metric space has unique fixed point.
Banach space: complete normed vector space.
Hilbert space: complete inner-product space.
Eigenfunction expansion: any function in L^2 space can be expanded as series of orthonormal eigenfunctions.
Poisson summation formula: Σ f(n) = Σ F(k), relating sum over function and its Fourier transform.
Dirac delta function δ(x), distribution with ∫ δ(x) f(x) dx = f(0).
Schwartz space: space of rapidly decreasing smooth functions.
Distribution theory: generalizes functions for differentiation and Fourier transform.
Cauchy sequence: a sequence {x_n} such that ∀ ε>0, ∃ N, m,n≥N ⇒ |x_m - x_n|<ε.
Taylor remainder: R_n(x) = f^(n+1)(ξ)/(n+1)! (x-a)^(n+1) for some ξ between a and x.
Implicit function theorem: if F(x,y)=0 and ∂F/∂y ≠ 0, then y can be locally solved as function of x.
Lagrange multipliers: optimize f(x,y,...) subject to g(x,y,...) = 0 by solving ∇f = λ ∇g.
Legendre polynomials P_n(x) are solutions to (1-x^2)y'' - 2xy' + n(n+1)y = 0.
Hermite polynomials H_n(x) satisfy H'' - 2xH' + 2nH = 0.
Chebyshev polynomials T_n(x) satisfy (1-x^2)y'' - xy' + n^2 y = 0.

Contrastive learning trains representations by distinguishing similar and dissimilar pairs of data.
Attention mechanisms can be applied to images to improve visual recognition tasks.
Variational autoencoders model latent variables with a probabilistic encoder-decoder architecture.

Phase transitions occur when a system changes from one state of matter to another, e.g., solid to liquid.
Reinforcement learning with policy gradients optimizes stochastic policies directly.
Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks solve vanishing gradient problems in RNNs.
The Fourier series represents periodic functions as sums of sines and cosines.
Schrödinger equation predicts how the quantum state of a physical system evolves over time.

Boltzmann machines learn probability distributions over binary-valued data using energy-based modeling.
The PageRank algorithm ranks web pages based on link structure and eigenvector centrality.
The backpropagation algorithm efficiently computes gradients for multilayer neural networks.
Stochastic gradient descent optimizes model parameters by computing gradients on mini-batches of data.
Capsule networks aim to preserve hierarchical pose relationships between features.
Elastic weight consolidation reduces catastrophic forgetting in continual learning.
Deep Q-Networks combine deep learning with Q-learning for end-to-end reinforcement learning.
Hopfield networks store associative memories and retrieve them via energy minimization.
Bayesian neural networks represent uncertainty by placing distributions over weights.
The Ising model explains phase transitions in ferromagnetic materials using spin interactions.
Diffusion models generate data by reversing a gradual noising process to produce realistic samples.
Neural style transfer modifies images by recombining content and style representations in deep networks.
The Hopf fibration maps S^3 to S^2, providing a topological structure with fibered circles.
Convolutional neural networks apply spatial filters to exploit local correlations in data.
Attention is used in vision transformers to model relationships between image patches.
Reversible neural networks allow exact reconstruction of inputs from outputs, saving memory in training.
Attention biasing can improve performance in multi-modal transformer architectures.
Neural ODEs unify residual networks and continuous-time dynamical systems for modeling trajectories.
Physics-informed neural networks encode differential equation constraints into deep learning models.
Hyperbolic neural networks use hyperbolic geometry to embed hierarchical data efficiently.
Quantum annealing leverages quantum tunneling to find low-energy states of combinatorial optimization problems.
Generative adversarial networks (GANs) train a generator and discriminator in a minimax game to synthesize realistic data.
Meta-learning enables models to adapt quickly to new tasks using prior experience across tasks.
Transformer architectures rely on self-attention mechanisms to capture dependencies without recurrence.
Layer normalization stabilizes training in deep neural networks by normalizing activations across features.
Graph neural networks extend deep learning to non-Euclidean data by propagating information over edges.
Neural architecture search (NAS) automates the design of optimal neural network architectures.
Dropout prevents overfitting by randomly deactivating neurons during training.
Fourier neural operators learn mappings between function spaces for solving PDEs.
Neural tangent kernels approximate training dynamics of infinitely wide neural networks.
Attention rollout analyzes layerwise attention contributions to model outputs.
Optimal transport theory measures the most efficient transformation between probability distributions.
Neural fields model continuous signals by representing them as learnable functions over space.
Contrastive predictive coding learns representations by predicting future latent embeddings.
Neural ODE-RNN hybrids combine continuous-time ODE modeling with sequential RNNs for time series.
Fourier feature embeddings improve the ability of neural networks to learn high-frequency functions.
Attention pooling aggregates token-level features using learned attention weights for global representations.
Implicit neural representations encode objects or signals as continuous functions parameterized by networks.
Diffusion probabilistic models iteratively denoise data to model complex distributions.
Equivariant neural networks preserve symmetries of input data through layers.
Physics-informed GANs incorporate physical constraints into generative adversarial frameworks.
Neural implicit surfaces represent 3D objects as continuous functions for high-resolution reconstruction.
Graph attention networks use attention mechanisms over graph nodes to improve representation learning.
Variational graph autoencoders learn latent representations for graph-structured data.
Siren networks employ periodic activation functions for representing complex signals and implicit functions.
Transformer-based vision models divide images into patches and apply self-attention for global context.
Score-based generative models learn gradients of log probability to sample complex distributions.
Contrastive language-image pretraining aligns visual and textual embeddings for multimodal understanding.
Neural fields for audio represent sound as continuous functions parameterized by networks.
Fourier neural operators can solve high-dimensional PDEs faster than traditional numerical methods.
Masked autoencoders pretrain vision transformers by reconstructing missing patches of input images.
Energy-based models define probability distributions through an energy function, optimized during training.
Neural operator learning generalizes classical numerical solvers by learning solution operators for PDEs.
A method for manufacturing semiconductor devices comprising: providing a substrate; depositing a dielectric layer; patterning the dielectric layer to form a trench; filling the trench with copper.
A combustion engine comprising: a cylinder; a piston reciprocating within the cylinder; a spark plug; and a fuel injector configured to inject fuel directly into the cylinder.
Method for creating microchips: deposit layers, etch, and dope with ions.
How to change a car tire: 1. Loosen lug nuts. 2. Jack up the car. 3. Remove lug nuts and tire. 4. Mount spare tire. 5. Tighten lug nuts. 6. Lower car. 7. Final tighten.
How to boil an egg: Place egg in pot, cover with water, bring to boil, turn off heat, cover, let sit for 9-12 minutes, then ice bath.
How to assemble a bookshelf: Unpack, attach sides, screw shelves, tighten screws.
A method for producing composite materials: layering fiber sheets with resin, curing under heat and pressure.
Design of a wind turbine blade with optimized aerofoil shape for maximum lift-to-drag ratio.
A hydraulic press comprising: a cylinder; a piston; a fluid reservoir; and a pressure regulation system.
Method for 3D printing metal parts: layer-by-layer deposition using selective laser melting.
Process for refining crude oil: distillation, catalytic cracking, and hydroprocessing.
Method for producing glass: melting silica with fluxing agents at high temperature, then controlled cooling.
Automotive braking system comprising disc brakes, hydraulic lines, and ABS control unit.
Aircraft wing design: tapered planform with winglets to reduce induced drag.
Solar panel manufacturing: layering photovoltaic cells, encapsulant, and glass cover.
Method for desalination: reverse osmosis through semi-permeable membranes.
Concrete formulation: cement, aggregates, water, and admixtures for specific strength and durability.
Robotic arm control system: servo motors, feedback sensors, and trajectory planning algorithms.
Pipeline design: steel tubing, pressure regulation valves, and corrosion inhibitors.
Water treatment system: filtration, UV sterilization, and chemical dosing.
Battery pack assembly: lithium-ion cells, thermal management, and protection circuitry.
Electric motor manufacturing: rotor winding, stator lamination, and insulation.
HVAC system design: ductwork layout, airflow optimization, and energy-efficient components.
Hydroelectric turbine: blade design, rotational mechanics, and generator coupling.
Chemical reactor design: temperature and pressure control, mixing, and reaction kinetics.
Additive manufacturing of polymers: layer deposition and curing.
Fiber optic cable assembly: glass fibers, cladding, and protective jacket.
Method for manufacturing electronic sensors: deposition, patterning, and encapsulation.
Mechanical gear assembly: shafts, bearings, and lubrication system.
Pneumatic actuator system: cylinder, piston, and pressure control valves.
Method for producing reinforced concrete: steel rebar placement, concrete pouring, and curing.
High-pressure gas pipeline: steel tubing, safety valves, and monitoring sensors.
Advanced battery recycling methods: separation of lithium, cobalt, and nickel using hydrometallurgical processes.
AI-based structural health monitoring: using sensors and deep learning to detect cracks and stress points in bridges.
Self-healing concrete incorporating microcapsules containing healing agents that release upon crack formation.
Additive manufacturing of metals with in-situ alloying for optimized mechanical properties.
Robust control of autonomous drones using sensor fusion and predictive modeling for obstacle avoidance.
Nanostructured coatings for corrosion resistance in marine environments, optimized via electrochemical deposition.
Energy-efficient HVAC system using adaptive control and variable-speed compressors.
Automated pipeline inspection using robotics and computer vision for leak detection.
High-temperature superconducting cables for power transmission with minimized resistive losses.
Carbon capture systems for industrial plants using amine-based absorption and regeneration cycles.
Hybrid powertrain optimization for electric and combustion engines using predictive energy management.
